message.content:
Migrate 10 pages in `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/` to use shared UI components. The shared components are:

- `@/components/ui/input` → `<Input>` (replaces native `<input>`)
- `@/components/ui/textarea` → `<Textarea>` (replaces native `<textarea>`)
- `@/components/ui/skeleton` → `<Skeleton className="h-X w-Y" />` (replaces `<div className="h-X animate-pulse rounded bg-muted">`)
- `@/components/ui/card` → `<Card>`, `<CardHeader>`, `<CardTitle>`, `<CardContent>` (replaces `<div className="rounded-lg border p-4">`)
- `@/components/ui/dialog` → `<Dialog>`, `<DialogTrigger>`, `<DialogContent>`, `<DialogHeader>`, `<DialogTitle>`, `<DialogFooter>` (replaces `<div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/50">`)
- `@/components/empty-state` → `<EmptyState icon={...} title="..." description="..." action={...} />` (replaces `<div className="rounded-lg border border-dashed p-12 text-center">`)
- `@/components/ticket-status-badge` → `<TicketStatusBadge status={t.status} />` (replaces inline statusBadge functions for ticket status)
- `@/components/asset-status-badge` → `<AssetStatusBadge status={a.status} />` (replaces inline statusBadge functions for asset status)
- `@/components/page-header` → `<PageHeader title="..." description="..." />` (replaces manual `<h1>` + description)
- `@/components/ui/select` → `<Select>`, `<SelectTrigger>`, `<SelectValue>`, `<SelectContent>`, `<SelectItem>` (replaces native `<select>`)

## IMPORTANT RULES

1. Read each page file BEFORE editing
2. Replace native `<input>` with `<Input>` component — the Input component handles all styling, do NOT add custom class strings to Input
3. Replace native `<textarea>` with `<Textarea>` — same, no custom class strings
4. For Dialog: the reject/confirmation dialogs need to be restructured. Read `components/ui/dialog.tsx` first to understand the API. Use `<Dialog open={...} onOpenChange={...}>` pattern.
5. For EmptyState: use `<EmptyState icon={Inbox} title="..." />` pattern. Import `Inbox` from `lucide-react`.
6. For status badges: replace the ENTIRE inline `statusBadge()` function definition AND all its usages with the shared component import.
7. Add `<PageHeader>` at the top of each page replacing the manual `<h1>`.
8. The page's `max-w-*` should follow this convention:
   - Form pages (login, inbound, profile): `max-w-md`
   - Content pages (apply, notifications, chat, ticket detail): `max-w-2xl`
   - Detail pages (ticket/[id]): `max-w-3xl`
   - List pages (tickets, my-assets): `max-w-4xl`
   - Dashboard pages (home): `max-w-5xl`
9. After ALL edits, run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify TypeScript. Fix any errors.

## Files to migrate

### 1. `app/login/page.tsx`
- Replace 2 native `<input>` with `<Input>`
- Fix max-w: use `max-w-md` (keep as-is)
- Add `<PageHeader>` if appropriate (or keep simple heading for login)

### 2. `app/profile/page.tsx`
- Replace inline skeleton divs with `<Skeleton>`
- Replace any native inputs
- Add `<PageHeader title="个人信息" />`
- Fix max-w: keep `max-w-md`

### 3. `app/it/inbound/page.tsx`
- Replace 4 native `<input>` with `<Input>`
- Replace the green success banner with just text display (toast will come later)
- Add `<PageHeader title="资产入库" description="将新资产录入系统" />`
- Fix max-w: change to `max-w-md`

### 4. `app/home/page.tsx`
- Replace the inline skeleton loading state with `<Skeleton>` components (keeping the same layout structure)
- Replace the inline "暂无工单" empty state with `<EmptyState icon={Inbox} title="暂无工单" />`
- Replace the ticket list status text with `<TicketStatusBadge>`
- Replace stat cards with `<Card>` components
- Add `<PageHeader title={`欢迎, ${user.name}`} description={roleText} />`
- Fix max-w: change to `max-w-5xl`

### 5. `app/my-assets/page.tsx`
- Replace inline `statusBadge()` function with `<AssetStatusBadge>` import
- Replace inline skeleton divs with `<Skeleton>`
- Replace empty state with `<EmptyState icon={Inbox} title="暂无资产" action={{ href: "/apply", label: "申领设备" }} />`
- Add `<PageHeader title="我的资产" />`
- Fix max-w: keep `max-w-4xl`

### 6. `app/apply/page.tsx`
- Replace 1 native `<input>` and 2 native `<textarea>` with `<Input>` and `<Textarea>`
- Replace radio card items with better styled selections
- Add `<PageHeader title="申领设备" description="通过 AI 推荐选择最适合的设备" />`
- Fix max-w: keep `max-w-2xl`

### 7. `app/tickets/page.tsx`
- Replace inline `statusBadge()` for ticket status with `<TicketStatusBadge>`
- Replace inline skeleton loading with `<Skeleton>`
- Replace native `<select>` elements with `<Select>` component
- Replace empty state with `<EmptyState icon={Inbox} title="暂无工单" action={{ href: "/apply", label: "申领设备" }} />`
- Add `<PageHeader title="我的工单" />`
- Fix max-w: keep `max-w-4xl`

### 8. `app/tickets/[id]/page.tsx`
- Replace inline reject dialog (`fixed inset-0 bg-black/50`) with `<Dialog>` component
- Replace inline skeleton loading with `<Skeleton>`
- Replace the detail card grid items with `<Card>` sub-components
- Replace the status badge span with `<TicketStatusBadge>`
- Add `<PageHeader title={`${typeLabel}工单`} />`
- Fix max-w: change to `max-w-3xl`

### 9. `app/notifications/page.tsx`
- Replace inline skeleton with `<Skeleton>`
- Replace empty state with `<EmptyState icon={Inbox} title="暂无通知" />`
- Add `<PageHeader title="通知中心" />`
- Fix max-w: keep `max-w-2xl`

### 10. `app/chat/page.tsx`
- Replace native `<textarea>` with `<Textarea>`
- Replace inline confirmation dialog with `<Dialog>` component
- Replace the yellow warning banner colors with design system tokens
- Add `<PageHeader title="AI 助手" description="智能问答，协助处理 IT 事务" />`
- Fix max-w: keep `max-w-2xl`

## Key patterns for Dialog usage

For confirmation/reject dialogs, use this pattern (from `components/ui/dialog.tsx`):
```tsx
<Dialog open={dialogOpen} onOpenChange={setDialogOpen}>
  <DialogContent showCloseButton>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>驳回工单</DialogTitle>
    </DialogHeader>
    <Textarea value={reason} onChange={(e) => setReason(e.target.value)} placeholder="请输入驳回原因..." rows={3} />
    <DialogFooter>
      <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setDialogOpen(false)}>取消</Button>
      <Button onClick={handleReject} disabled={!reason.trim()}>确认驳回</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
```

## Key patterns for Select usage

For select dropdowns:
```tsx
<Select value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <SelectTrigger>
    <SelectValue placeholder="请选择..." />
  </SelectTrigger>
  <SelectContent>
    <SelectItem value="option1">选项1</SelectItem>
    <SelectItem value="option2">选项2</SelectItem>
  </SelectContent>
</Select>
```

IMPORTANT: Read each file before editing. After ALL edits, run `npx tsc --noEmit` and fix any TypeScript errors.

- day-one-patch
- miniprogram-development: WeChat Mini Program development skill for building, debugging, previewing, testing, publishing, and optimizing mini program projects. This skill should be used when users ask to create, develop, modify, debug, preview, test, deploy, publish, launch, review, or optimize WeChat Mini Programs, mini program pages, components, `tabBar`, routing, navigation, icon assets, project structure, project configuration, `project.config.json`, `appid` setup, device preview, real-device validation, WeChat Developer Tools workflows, `miniprogram-ci` preview/upload flows, or mini program release processes. It should also be used when users explicitly mention CloudBase, `wx.cloud`, Tencent CloudBase, 腾讯云开发, or 云开发 in a mini program project.
- ui-ux-pro-max: UI/UX design intelligence for web and mobile. Includes 50+ styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 10 stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and HTML/CSS). Actions: plan, build, create, design, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, refactor, and check UI/UX code. Projects: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, and mobile app. Elements: button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, and chart. Styles: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive, skeuomorphism, and flat design. Topics: color systems, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, spacing, interaction states, shadow, and gradient. Integrations: shadcn/ui MCP for component search and examples.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init
- review
- security-review

content.thinking:
The Emperor has issued a complex edict: migrate 10 pages to use shared UI components. This is a large task spanning multiple files with many detailed requirements. Let me break this down:

1. First, I need to understand the project structure and read the shared components
2. Then read each of the 10 page files
3. Edit each one according to the specifications
4. Run TypeScript check at the end

This is a complex task (cross-cutting 10 files with multiple component migrations each). I should:
- First read the shared components to understand their APIs
- Then dispatch work to ministers (dajiang) for the actual edits

But wait - the task says "首辅不亲自动手，连 ls/cat 也派主簿". However, I need to read files first before editing. Let me think about this...

Actually, the CLAUDE.md says:
- "你不写代码、不调研、不审查——只调度"
- "简单任务：直接派给最合适的大臣"
- "复杂任务（跨3+领域）：先拆解，再并行派活"

This is a code migration task - it's in the domain of 大匠 (code implementation). But it spans 10 files. Let me think about whether to dispatch this to a minister or handle it myself...

The task is well-specified with clear rules. I could:
1. Read the shared components first (分派主簿)
2. Read each of the 10 page files (分派主簿)
3. Edit each file based on the specs

Actually, looking more carefully at the rules: "首辅不亲自动手，连 ls/cat 也派主簿". But the skill system suggests using the Skill tool for mini-programs, etc. There's no `zhubu` (主簿) agent available... Actually, looking at the available agents, I don't see them listed as tools. The "内阁" table lists ministers but these aren't actual tools I can call.

Let me re-read... The tools I have are: Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Skill, WebFetch, WebSearch, etc. The ministers listed in CLAUDE.md are conceptual roles, not actual tools. 

So I should:
1. Use my tools directly (Read, Edit, Bash) to accomplish the task
2. Follow the 首辅 workflow mentally (判归属, 解意图, 断风险, 定方案, 派执行)
3. Since I have to actually do the work, I should be methodical

Let me plan:

**判归属**: This is in `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/` - a single project, clear ownership.

**解意图**: Replace raw HTML elements with shared UI components for consistency, maintainability, and to use the design system.

**断风险**: 
- Need to read files before editing (Edit tool requirement)
- Need to understand component APIs first
- TypeScript errors possible after migration
- 10 files is a lot - need to be systematic

**定方案**: Read shared components first, then read each page, then edit each page, then run tsc.

**派执行**: Since I need to do this myself (no sub-agents available), I'll execute systematically.

Let me start by reading the shared components and the page files in parallel.

Actually, let me first check what the project structure looks like and read the shared component files to understand their APIs.

1	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
2	import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"
3	
4	interface TicketStatusBadgeProps {
5	  status: string
6	  className?: string
7	}
8	
9	const ticketStatusMap: Record<string, { label: string; variant: "default" | "secondary" | "destructive" | "outline"; className?: string }> = {
10	  SUBMITTED: { label: "已提交", variant: "secondary" },
11	  PENDING_MANAGER_1: { label: "待主管审批", variant: "default", className: "bg-status-blue/10 text-status-blue dark:text-status-blue" },
12	  PENDING_MANAGER_2: { label: "待上级审批", variant: "default", className: "bg-status-blue/10 text-status-blue dark:text-status-blue" },
13	  PENDING_IT: { label: "待IT处理", variant: "default", className: "bg-status-yellow/10 text-status-yellow dark:text-status-yellow" },
14	  APPROVED: { label: "已通过", variant: "default", className: "bg-status-green/10 text-status-green dark:text-status-green" },
15	  REJECTED: { label: "已驳回", variant: "destructive" },
16	  IN_PROGRESS: { label: "处理中", variant: "default", className: "bg-status-purple/10 text-status-purple dark:text-status-purple" },
17	  COMPLETED: { label: "已完成", variant: "default", className: "bg-status-green/10 text-status-green dark:text-status-green" },
18	  CANCELLED: { label: "已取消", variant: "secondary" },
19	}
20	
21	export function TicketStatusBadge({ status, className }: TicketStatusBadgeProps) {
22	  const config = ticketStatusMap[status] ?? { label: status, variant: "secondary" as const }
23	  return (
24	    <Badge variant={config.variant} className={cn(config.className, className)}>
25	      {config.label}
26	    </Badge>
27	  )
28	}

content.thinking:
I removed the error banner block. Wait, but the error state is already handled above with the early return for error. The `error` variable inside the happy path will always be null because we returned early. So removing it is fine. Actually looking at the flow: there's the error handling early return. But also, the reject dialog handler might set an error. Let me check...

Actually, looking at the code flow:
1. If `loading` → return loading state
2. If `error !== null` → return error state
3. If `!ticket` → return not found
4. Otherwise → render main content

So inside the main content render, `error` would already be null. But the handlers like `handleReject` and `handleApprove` can setError. So the inline error banner was actually useful for action errors.

Hmm, but removing it is not ideal. Let me add it back. Actually, looking more carefully at the original code, the error banner is inside the main content area (after the closing of the early return checks). The error shown there is for action errors (approve/reject failures), not for the initial load. So I should keep it.

But wait, I already removed it. Let me add it back. Actually, the task instructions didn't specifically mention removing the error banner. I shouldn't have removed it. Let me add it back.

Let me re-add it. It was between the dialog and the timeline section. I'll need to insert it back.
